examples.title: C: Semaphore for Mutual Exclusion
Python: Semaphore for Mutual Exclusion
examples.code:
import threading

# Python's threading.Semaphore
mutex = threading.Semaphore(1) # Binary semaphore
counter = 0

def increment():
 global counter
 for _ in range(100000):
 mutex.acquire() # wait (P)
 counter += 1 # Critical section
 mutex.release() # signal (V)

t1 = threading.Thread(target=increment)
t2 = threading.Thread(target=increment)

t1.start()
t2.start()
t1.join()
t2.join()

print(f"Counter: {counter} (expected: 200000)")

examples.title: Java: Semaphore for Mutual Exclusion
C: Counting Semaphore for Resource Pool
Python: Counting Semaphore for Resource Pool
examples.code:
import threading
import time

NUM_RESOURCES = 3
NUM_THREADS = 5

# Counting semaphore
resources = threading.Semaphore(NUM_RESOURCES)

def use_resource(thread_id):
 print(f"Thread {thread_id}: Waiting for resource")
 resources.acquire()
 
 print(f"Thread {thread_id}: Using resource")
 time.sleep(1) # Simulate usage
 
 print(f"Thread {thread_id}: Releasing resource")
 resources.release()

threads = []
for i in range(NUM_THREADS):
 t = threading.Thread(target=use_resource, args=(i,))
 threads.append(t)
 t.start()

for t in threads:
 t.join()

print("All threads completed")

examples.title: Java: Counting Semaphore for Resource Pool
C: Semaphore for Ordering (Synchronization)
Python: Semaphore for Ordering (Synchronization)
examples.code:
import threading
import time

# Semaphore initialized to 0 for ordering
sync_sem = threading.Semaphore(0)

def task_A():
 print("A: Doing work (S1)")
 time.sleep(0.5)
 print("A: Signaling B")
 sync_sem.release() # Signal B

def task_B():
 print("B: Waiting for A's signal")
 sync_sem.acquire() # Wait for A
 print("B: Received signal, doing work (S2)")

# Start B first, but S2 will only run after S1
tB = threading.Thread(target=task_B)
tA = threading.Thread(target=task_A)

tB.start()
tA.start()

tA.join()
tB.join()

print("Order guaranteed: S1 before S2")
